chapter_title.ar: باب وَصِيَّةِ الْغُلَامِ وَ الْجَارِيَةِ الَّتِي لَمْ تُدْرِكْ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهَا وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – Bequest of the boy and the maid who has not attained awareness (puberty), and what is allowed from it, and is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/47)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/47)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْغُلَامَ إِذَا حَضَرَهُ الْمَوْتُ فَأَوْصَى وَ لَمْ يُدْرِكْ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ لِذَوِي الْأَرْحَامِ وَ لَمْ تَجُزْ لِلْغُرَبَاءِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, form Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Al Noman, from Abu Ayoub, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The boy, when the death presents itself to him, so he bequeaths and is not aware (attained puberty), his bequest is allowed for the relatives and is not allowed for the strangers’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn al-Nu’man from abu Ayyub from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has narrated the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘When a boy is about to die his will is permissible in favor of the relatives but not in favor of the strangers.’”
